title: fritillaria meleagris subsp. burnatii
intro: Fritillaria meleagris subsp. burnatii, also known as Burnat's fritillary, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the lily family that is native to the mountainous regions of Europe. This plant contains a compound called galantamine, which has been found to possess medicinal properties, including antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Fritillaria meleagris subsp. burnatii include its potential use in treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis, and its ability to act as an expectorant to help clear mucus from the lungs. In herbal preparations, the plant's bulbs are typically dried and used in teas, tinctures, or capsules to harness its medicinal properties.
